steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the passage supports about OFT. The key sentence is: the discrepancy between the two species — jerboas avoiding high lunar intensity while lemurs seek it — 'is explicable in terms of OFT.' OFT explains why different species respond differently to the same circumstance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'can account for observations of different species responding differently to similar ecological circumstances': This is exactly what the passage says — OFT explains the discrepancy between jerboas and lemurs reacting oppositely to lunar intensity. ✓
  • Choice B — 'tends to allow for a better understanding of the benefits of ecological circumstances than the costs': The passage never compares OFT's ability to explain benefits versus costs. ✗
  • Choice C — 'may be weakened by the finding that costs and benefits vary by species': The opposite is true — OFT specifically accounts for variation by species. ✗
  • Choice D — 'can explain why some species act in accordance with cost-benefit trade-offs and others do not': The passage shows both species acting in accordance with OFT, not some acting against it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
